findings.description: Requirement observability.3 and harness-adapter.1 mandate that trace_llm_call include tokens. However, the @traced_harness decorator wraps the invoke() method which returns only a string (src/assistant/harnesses/base.py:34), shedding the token usage information from the underlying LLM response. Consequently, src/assistant/telemetry/decorators.py:127-142 hardcodes input_tokens=None and output_tokens=None.
findings.resolution: Change SdkHarnessAdapter.invoke to return a HarnessResult object containing both the response string and token counts, then update the @traced_harness decorator to extract and pass these tokens to trace_llm_call.
findings.title: traced_harness decorator fails to capture token counts

findings.description: LangfuseProvider.trace_* methods use start_as_current_observation() after the operation is already complete. The SDK measures duration from the start of the context manager to its end, resulting in an effectively zero-duration span in the Langfuse UI. The duration_ms argument is only recorded as a metadata attribute (src/assistant/telemetry/providers/langfuse.py:92-104).
findings.resolution: In LangfuseProvider, calculate start_time and end_time from the current time and duration_ms, then pass them to start_as_current_observation() to ensure Langfuse records the correct wall-clock duration.
findings.title: Langfuse span duration reflects provider latency rather than operation duration

findings.description: NoopProvider uses **kwargs for trace_* methods (e.g. src/assistant/telemetry/providers/noop.py:40), whereas the ObservabilityProvider Protocol and LangfuseProvider define explicit keyword-only arguments. This allows callers to omit required arguments (like tool_kind or duration_ms) on the noop path without a TypeError, leading to runtime failures only when Langfuse is enabled.
findings.resolution: Update all trace_* methods in NoopProvider to match the explicit keyword-only signatures defined in the ObservabilityProvider Protocol.
findings.title: NoopProvider fails to enforce Protocol required arguments

findings.description: TelemetryConfig.from_env() emits a warning directly via logger.warning (src/assistant/telemetry/config.py:97-103). This bypasses the _warn_once() deduplication tracker in factory.py, potentially leading to repeated warnings if the config is loaded outside the singleton factory path.
findings.resolution: Remove the warning from config.py. Instead, set a flag in TelemetryConfig and have get_observability_provider() in factory.py emit the warning using _warn_once().
findings.title: TelemetryConfig warning bypasses factory deduplication mechanism

findings.description: LangfuseProvider.trace_llm_call passes md to start_as_current_observation, then immediately calls obs.update(metadata=md) (src/assistant/telemetry/providers/langfuse.py:126-140). This is redundant in the Langfuse v3 SDK and adds unnecessary overhead.
findings.resolution: Remove the redundant obs.update(metadata=md) calls from trace_llm_call, trace_delegation, trace_tool_call, and trace_memory_op.
findings.title: Redundant metadata update in LangfuseProvider

findings.description: The module-level __getattr__ in langfuse.py (line 262) merely raises AttributeError(name), which is the default Python behavior for missing attributes on a module. It provides no functional benefit.
findings.title: Dead module-level __getattr__ in langfuse.py

findings.description: The 'Claude Code Stop hook wiring' section in docs/observability.md starts abruptly. A brief introductory sentence explaining the purpose of the hook (session-level tracing) would improve UX.
findings.resolution: Add an introductory sentence: 'You can correlate CLI spans with full Claude Code sessions by wiring a Stop hook.'
findings.title: Improve clarity of Claude Code Stop hook documentation
